tasks.subject: [mcp-local-auth-posture] [P1] mcp-local-token.ts substrate (resolve / rotate / cached accessor + path) + unit tests
tasks.description: Create apps/server/src/services/core/auth/mcp-local-token.ts — a near-clone of the sibling apps/server/src/services/core/auth/secret.ts that resolves this instance's per-instance local MCP token. TSDoc on every export (Hard Rule 4). Constants: TOKEN_FILE_NAME = 'mcp-local-token'; TOKEN_FILE_MODE = 0o600; TOKEN_PREFIX = 'dork_mcp_local_'; TOKEN_BYTES = 32. Module-level cache: let cachedToken: string | null = null and let cachedPath: string | null = null. Exports: (a) resolveMcpLocalToken(dorkHome: string): string | null — precedence exactly like secret.ts: (1) if env.MCP_API_KEY is set (import env from '../../../env.js'; treat whitespace-only as unset) the env key IS the bearer clients use, so return null and neither read nor write the file; (2) read <dorkHome>/mcp-local-token if it exists (fs.readFileSync utf8, trim; if non-empty, run repairTokenPermissions, set cachedToken + cachedPath, return it); ENOENT is the expected first-boot case, any other read error is logged then falls through to generate; (3) generate TOKEN_PREFIX + randomBytes(32).toString('hex') (64 hex chars), fs.mkdirSync(dorkHome, { recursive: true }), fs.writeFileSync(path, value, { mode: 0o600 }), fs.chmodSync(path, 0o600) (umask defense — writeFileSync mode is ignored if the file exists and is subject to umask on create), log ONLY the path (never the value), set cachedToken + cachedPath, return it. (b) rotateMcpLocalToken(dorkHome: string): string — always generate a fresh TOKEN_PREFIX + hex, write 0o600 (overwriting), chmod 0o600, update cachedToken + cachedPath, return the new value. (c) getMcpLocalToken(): string | null — return cachedToken (the middleware and the config DTO compare against this without a per-request file read). (d) getMcpLocalTokenPath(): string | null — return cachedPath (the middleware's helpful 401 body names this file path without knowing dorkHome). (e) private repairTokenPermissions(tokenPath: string): void — copy the shape of secret.ts:124-142 verbatim: no-op on process.platform === 'win32'; else stat the mode, and if (mode & 0o077) !== 0 chmod back to 0o600 and logger.warn (a stat/chmod failure must never throw — warn and continue). Never log the token value; only the path. Write apps/server/src/services/core/auth/__tests__/mcp-local-token.test.ts mirroring apps/server/src/services/core/auth/__tests__/secret.test.ts, using a fresh tmpdir per case for dorkHome (each case a purpose comment, no always-pass): env override wins (env.MCP_API_KEY set → resolveMcpLocalToken returns null and writes no file); first boot generates + persists (file exists, mode is 0o600, value starts with 'dork_mcp_local_' and the suffix is 64 hex chars); the persisted file is read stably across two resolve calls (same value); lax permissions are repaired (pre-create the file 0o644, resolve, assert it is re-tightened to 0o600 and a warn was emitted); rotateMcpLocalToken writes a new DISTINCT value and getMcpLocalToken() then returns the rotated value; getMcpLocalToken()/getMcpLocalTokenPath() return the cached value/path after resolve. Acceptance: the module compiles standalone; all unit tests pass; token format is 'dork_mcp_local_' + 64 hex. Verify: pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/services/core/auth/__tests__/mcp-local-token.test.ts && pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 1 (the local token substrate) + §Testing Strategy (Unit — token substrate); precedent apps/server/src/services/core/auth/secret.ts.
tasks.activeForm: Building the local MCP token substrate and its unit tests

tasks.subject: [mcp-local-auth-posture] [P1] Resolve the local token once at server boot (login-off + no MCP_API_KEY)
tasks.description: Wire the token resolution into the server boot sequence so getMcpLocalToken() is populated before the first request. In apps/server/src/index.ts, in the same auth boot region as initAuth(db, dorkHome) (~line 242) and void seedLegacyMcpApiKey(db) (~line 249), resolve the local token ONCE — but ONLY when login is off (configManager.get('auth')?.enabled !== true) AND env.MCP_API_KEY is unset. In that case call resolveMcpLocalToken(dorkHome) (import from './services/core/auth/mcp-local-token.js', or re-export it through the auth barrel './services/core/auth/index.js' and import from there for consistency with initAuth/seedLegacyMcpApiKey), which generates + persists the 0600 file on first boot and populates the module cache that getMcpLocalToken()/getMcpLocalTokenPath() read. When MCP_API_KEY is set (env override is the bearer) or login is on (per-user keys, ADR-0320), do NOT resolve — leave the cache null so the middleware's local-token acceptor stays inert in those modes. resolveMcpLocalToken already logs only the file path on generation; add no line that logs the value. This is a security-ordering dependency for task 2.2: the middleware's local-token acceptor compares against getMcpLocalToken(), so this boot wiring MUST be present before the passthrough is deleted — otherwise getMcpLocalToken() returns null, the acceptor never matches, and a legitimate client presenting the token would be hard-locked with a 401. Acceptance: server typecheck + build pass; the resolve call is gated on login-off AND no MCP_API_KEY; nothing logs the token value. (Runtime behavior — file created 0600 on login-off boot, no file when MCP_API_KEY set — is verified end-to-end by the task 2.3 integration test; here confirm compile.) Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 1 (Generation timing — resolved once during server startup, only when login is off and MCP_API_KEY is unset). Depends on 1.1 (the module); sequential before 2.2 (both edit index.ts and 2.2 needs this cache populated).
tasks.activeForm: Wiring local-token resolution into server boot

tasks.subject: [mcp-local-auth-posture] [P2] READ_ONLY_MCP_TOOL_NAMES SSOT + drift-guard test vs live tools/list
tasks.description: Create apps/server/src/services/core/external-mcp/tool-security.ts exporting READ_ONLY_MCP_TOOL_NAMES: ReadonlySet<string> (TSDoc, Hard Rule 4) — the single source of truth for the login-off /mcp read-only carve-out. It contains EXACTLY these 23 tool names (the readOnlyHint===true set enumerated by the readOnlyHint audit in §readOnlyHint audit): ping, get_server_info, get_session_count, get_agent, tasks_list, tasks_get_run_history, binding_list, get_extension_api, list_extensions, get_extension_errors, mesh_list, mesh_status, mesh_inspect, mesh_query_topology, relay_list_endpoints, relay_list_adapters, relay_get_trace, relay_get_metrics, marketplace_search, marketplace_get, marketplace_recommend, marketplace_list_marketplaces, marketplace_list_installed. Build it as new Set([...]) typed ReadonlySet<string>. Document that any tool name NOT in this set is treated as GUARDED — an added tool defaults to token-required (fail-closed). Co-located under services/core/external-mcp/ so the middleware and the drift test both import it. Write the drift-guard test apps/server/src/services/core/external-mcp/__tests__/tool-security.test.ts (create the __tests__ dir): instantiate createExternalMcpServer (from '../../mcp-server.js') with a mock McpToolDeps AND marketplace deps — mirror the createMinimalDeps() harness in apps/server/src/routes/__tests__/mcp-integration.test.ts, and CRITICALLY pass the marketplace deps too so the 5 marketplace read-only tools (marketplace_search/get/recommend/list_marketplaces/list_installed) actually register (mcp-server.ts:88 skips them when marketplaceDeps is undefined — without them the count is 18, not 23). Connect the server to a StreamableHTTPServerTransport (as mcp-integration.test.ts does) or an in-memory transport, issue a tools/list request, and collect each tool's annotations.readOnlyHint. Assert BOTH directions (each a purpose comment, no always-pass): (a) every tool returned with annotations.readOnlyHint === true is present in READ_ONLY_MCP_TOOL_NAMES; (b) every name in READ_ONLY_MCP_TOOL_NAMES appears in the tools/list result with readOnlyHint === true; (c) READ_ONLY_MCP_TOOL_NAMES.size === 23. This guarantees the constant can never silently drift from the annotations it mirrors. Acceptance: tool-security.ts + the drift test compile and pass; the set has exactly 23 members equal to the live read-only tools. Verify: pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/services/core/external-mcp/__tests__/tool-security.test.ts && pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck. Cites: spec §'The read-only carve-out SSOT — READ_ONLY_MCP_TOOL_NAMES', §readOnlyHint audit (the 23 names), §Testing Strategy (carve-out drift guard). Independent of P1 (new file, no shared edits) — runs in parallel with 1.1/1.2; blocks 2.2 (the middleware imports the set).
tasks.activeForm: Building the read-only carve-out SSOT and drift-guard test

tasks.subject: [mcp-local-auth-posture] [P2] createMcpAuth({surface}) rewrite: delete passthrough + local-token acceptor + carve-out + helpful 401s + rewire 3 mounts + full matrix tests (ATOMIC)
tasks.description: SECURITY-CRITICAL ATOMIC TASK — the passthrough deletion, the local-token acceptor, the read-only carve-out, the three mount re-wirings, and the full middleware matrix tests MUST all land in this ONE task/commit. The workspace must never sit in a state where the passthrough is gone but the local-token acceptor is not wired (legit clients hard-locked by 401) NOR where the acceptor exists but the passthrough still allows tokenless mutating calls (surface still open). Rewrite apps/server/src/middleware/mcp-auth.ts: replace the exported async function mcpApiKeyAuth with a factory createMcpAuth({ surface }: { surface: 'mcp' | 'a2a' }) that returns an async (req, res, next) middleware; keep the constantTimeEquals and extractBearerToken helpers. Common acceptors run first on BOTH surfaces, allow on first match: (1) env.MCP_API_KEY exact constant-time match; (2) await verifyRequestAuth(req) identity (per-user Better Auth key / session — the login-on path); (3) legacy configManager.get('mcp')?.apiKey exact constant-time match; (4) NEW — local token exact constant-time match against getMcpLocalToken() (import from '../services/core/auth/mcp-local-token.js'), ONLY when login is off (configManager.get('auth')?.enabled !== true) AND getMcpLocalToken() is non-null. DELETE the old branch-4 passthrough entirely (current mcp-auth.ts:63-68, the `if (!envKey && !legacyKey && !authEnabled) next()`). If no acceptor matched: LOGIN ON (config.auth.enabled === true) → 401 with the surface-shaped body, no tokenless path, local token never consulted. LOGIN OFF, surface 'mcp' → peek req.body (the parsed JSON-RPC message; express.json({limit:'1mb'}) is mounted app-wide at app.ts:105 before the /mcp mount, so req.body is available) and apply the capability carve-out: a discovery/handshake method (initialize, ping, tools/list, resources/list, resources/templates/list, prompts/list, or any notifications/*) → allow; a tools/call whose params.name is in READ_ONLY_MCP_TOOL_NAMES (import from '../services/core/external-mcp/tool-security.js') → allow; a tools/call on any other or unknown tool → 401; any other method (resources/read, prompts/get, completion/*, unknown) → 401 (fail-closed); a JSON-RPC batch (array body) → allow only if EVERY element is independently allowed, otherwise 401 for the whole batch; an unparseable or missing body → 401. LOGIN OFF, surface 'a2a' → req.method === 'GET' (agent-card discovery, well-known + per-agent card) → allow; POST (JSON-RPC execution) → 401 (A2A execution is always token-gated; it has no read/write annotation to carve on). Helpful 401 bodies (writing-for-humans): /mcp keeps the JSON-RPC error shape { jsonrpc:'2.0', error:{ code:-32001, message }, id:null }; /a2a uses its error shape. The message NAMES the token file path (getMcpLocalTokenPath() from the token module, e.g. <dorkHome>/mcp-local-token) and the Authorization: Bearer header, and points at Settings → Server → External MCP — e.g. 'Unauthorized. This DorkOS tool needs your local MCP token. Find it in Settings → Server → External MCP, or in <path>, and send it as "Authorization: Bearer <token>".' NEVER echo the token value. Extract small helpers (isDiscoveryMethod, classifyMcpMessage, isAllowedMcpMessage) so cyclomatic complexity stays under 15 and the single-message and batch paths share one classifier. Re-wire the three mounts in apps/server/src/index.ts: the /mcp mount (~line 806) uses createMcpAuth({ surface: 'mcp' }); the two well-known agent-card GETs (~lines 1036-1037) use createMcpAuth({ surface: 'a2a' }); the /a2a mount (~line 1040) uses createMcpAuth({ surface: 'a2a' }). Update the import at index.ts:100 (mcpApiKeyAuth → createMcpAuth). Leave the /codex-ui-mcp stub mount (~line 825, deliberately no auth middleware) unchanged. Extend apps/server/src/middleware/__tests__/mcp-auth.test.ts to drive the FULL matrix — extend the existing createMockReq/createMockRes/mockConfig harness and add req.body + req.method to createMockReq; mock getMcpLocalToken to a known value. Cases (each a purpose comment, no always-pass): surface 'mcp' login-off — discovery methods (initialize/ping/tools/list/resources/list/prompts/list/notifications) allow tokenless; a read-only tools/call (e.g. ping, marketplace_search) allows tokenless; a mutating tools/call (e.g. create_extension, relay_send) 401s tokenless and allows WITH the local token; resources/read 401s tokenless; an unknown method 401s tokenless; a mixed batch [read-only + mutating] 401s; an all-read-only batch allows; unparseable/empty body 401s; each acceptor (env key, legacy key, local token) authorizes a mutating call; the 401 body contains the token file path + 'Authorization: Bearer' and does NOT contain the token value. surface 'a2a' login-off — GET allows tokenless; POST 401s tokenless and allows with the token. Login ON (both surfaces) — unauthenticated 401s; env/user/legacy key allows; the local token is NOT accepted (inactive when login is on). Acceptance: mcp-auth.ts compiles; the 3 mounts use createMcpAuth with the correct surface; the passthrough is gone; the full matrix passes; server typecheck + lint pass. Verify: pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/middleware/__tests__/mcp-auth.test.ts && pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/server lint. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 3 (middleware rewrite + the compact logic table), §'Helpful 401 bodies', §Goals, §Acceptance Criteria. Depends on 1.2 (boot must have populated getMcpLocalToken — the security-ordering edge) and 2.1 (READ_ONLY_MCP_TOOL_NAMES). Runs in parallel with all of Phase 3.
tasks.activeForm: Rewriting the MCP auth middleware and rewiring the mounts

tasks.subject: [mcp-local-auth-posture] [P2] End-to-end integration tests through the real /mcp + middleware wiring
tasks.description: Prove the new posture end-to-end through the real /mcp mount with the live auth wired (createMcpAuth({ surface: 'mcp' })). Extend apps/server/src/routes/__tests__/mcp-integration.test.ts and apps/server/src/middleware/__tests__/mcp-auth.integration.test.ts, reusing the existing MCP integration harness (createExternalMcpServer + a fresh StreamableHTTPServerTransport per POST, as mcp-integration.test.ts already does) and a mocked/known local token via getMcpLocalToken. Cases (each a purpose comment, no always-pass): a tokenless read-only tools/call (e.g. ping / get_server_info / marketplace_search) succeeds (allowed by the carve-out); a tokenless discovery tools/list succeeds; a tokenless mutating tools/call (e.g. create_extension) 401s with the helpful body that names the token file path and the Authorization: Bearer header and does NOT echo the token value; the SAME mutating call WITH Authorization: Bearer <local token> succeeds; a tokenless resources/read 401s (fail-closed on data reads). For mcp-auth.integration.test.ts, mount the middleware into a minimal express app that also mounts express.json before the middleware, so req.body peeking behaves exactly as production. Acceptance: all integration cases pass; the helpful 401 body is asserted; the token-authenticated mutating call succeeds. Verify: pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/middleware/__tests__/mcp-auth.integration.test.ts apps/server/src/routes/__tests__/mcp-integration.test.ts. Cites: spec §Testing Strategy (Integration — end-to-end auth), §Acceptance Criteria (login-off /mcp). Depends on 2.2 (the real middleware + mounts). Runs in parallel with all of Phase 3.
tasks.activeForm: Writing the end-to-end MCP auth integration tests

tasks.subject: [mcp-local-auth-posture] [P3] ServerConfig.mcp DTO (authSource 'local-token' + localToken) + rotate route + OpenAPI regen + tests
tasks.description: Extend the ServerConfig RESPONSE DTO and add the rotate route (NO UserConfigSchema change, NO conf migration — the token is a 0600 file, not persisted config). DTO schema in packages/shared/src/schemas.ts (ServerConfigSchema.mcp, ~lines 2190-2212): extend authSource from z.enum(['env','user-keys','none']) to z.enum(['env','user-keys','none','local-token']) and update its .openapi({description}) to mention 'local-token' (login off, no MCP_API_KEY — gated by the per-instance local token); add localToken: z.string().nullable().optional() with .openapi({ description: 'The per-instance local MCP bearer token, present only when authSource is local-token (login off, no MCP_API_KEY); null otherwise' }). DTO population in apps/server/src/routes/config.ts (the mcp IIFE, ~lines 162-183): import getMcpLocalToken from the token module ('../services/core/auth/mcp-local-token.js'). Compute authSource with the local-token case inserted where the old code returned 'none' in login-off mode: envKey → 'env'; else if (legacyKey || hasAnyApiKey()) → 'user-keys'; else (login off, no env key, no legacy key, no user keys) → 'local-token' WHEN getMcpLocalToken() is non-null, otherwise 'none' (the degenerate can't-generate fallback that should not occur in normal boot). authConfigured stays authSource !== 'none' (so 'local-token' → true, the surface is gated). Add localToken: authSource === 'local-token' ? getMcpLocalToken() : null to the returned mcp object — emit the token ONLY in local-token mode, never in env/user-keys/none. Rotate route in apps/server/src/routes/config.ts (the config router is already mounted at /api/config in app.ts:120, so add a sub-route, no index.ts/app.ts edit): router.post('/mcp/rotate-token', ...) → POST /api/config/mcp/rotate-token. Thin handler (validate → service → response, per .claude/rules/server-structure.md): if env.MCP_API_KEY is set OR configManager.get('auth')?.enabled === true, return 409 with a clear error (the local token does not apply under an env override or login-on); otherwise resolve dorkHome (process.env.DORK_HOME is set at boot in index.ts:173, or call resolveDorkHome()), call rotateMcpLocalToken(dorkHome) — which regenerates + persists 0600 and refreshes the getMcpLocalToken() cache — and return 200 { localToken }. OpenAPI: run pnpm docs:export-api to regenerate docs/api/openapi.json so the ServerConfig.mcp additions land, keeping the openapi-fresh CI check (.github/workflows/docs-openapi-check.yml) green. Tests — extend apps/server/src/routes/__tests__/config-mcp.test.ts (each a purpose comment, no always-pass; mock getMcpLocalToken/rotateMcpLocalToken): login off + no MCP_API_KEY + local token present → GET /api/config returns mcp.authSource === 'local-token', authConfigured true, mcp.localToken equals the token; MCP_API_KEY set → authSource 'env', localToken null; login on (auth.enabled true) → authSource 'user-keys' (per existing precedence), localToken null; POST /api/config/mcp/rotate-token in login-off/no-env → 200 with a new distinct localToken; POST when MCP_API_KEY set → 409; POST when login on → 409. Acceptance: shared + server typecheck; config-mcp tests pass; docs/api/openapi.json regenerated (openapi-fresh green); UserConfigSchema UNCHANGED and no conf migration ships. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/shared build && pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck && pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/routes/__tests__/config-mcp.test.ts && pnpm docs:export-api. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 2 (NO persisted-config change), §Detailed Design 4 (Server DTO + rotate route), §Testing Strategy (Route — rotate + DTO), §Acceptance Criteria. Depends on 1.1 (getMcpLocalToken/rotateMcpLocalToken). Runs in parallel with all of Phase 2 (no shared files: schemas.ts + config.ts vs mcp-auth.ts + index.ts).
tasks.activeForm: Adding the config DTO fields and rotate route

tasks.subject: [mcp-local-auth-posture] [P3] ExternalMcpCard local-token display + paste-ready snippet + honest env/user-keys/none states + tests
tasks.description: Surface the local token honestly in the Server settings tab (display + honest states only — the Rotate button and its transport wiring land in task 3.3). Files: apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/external-mcp/ExternalMcpCard.tsx, apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/external-mcp/SetupInstructions.tsx, apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/lib/external-mcp-snippets.ts, plus tests. After task 3.1, McpConfig = NonNullable<ServerConfig['mcp']> carries authSource including 'local-token' and localToken: string | null. Pass the real token into the snippets: in ExternalMcpCard.tsx change the SetupInstructions call (currently line ~125, apiKey={null}) to apiKey={mcp.localToken ?? null} so buildSnippets pre-fills the Authorization: Bearer dork_mcp_local_… header instead of the dork_mcp_YOUR_API_KEY placeholder (buildSnippets already embeds a non-null apiKey — external-mcp-snippets.ts:41). In external-mcp-snippets.ts update only the placeholder fallback TSDoc/comment so it points a token-less user at Settings → Server → External MCP or the mcp-local-token file (keep the buildSnippets signature and the placeholder string). Extend McpAuthRow (in ExternalMcpCard.tsx, ~line 134) with a 'local-token' branch and pass localToken into it: render a KeyRound 'Local MCP token' row showing the token in a read-only field with a Copy button (CopyButton from '@/layers/shared/ui'), a short line explaining clients send it as a Bearer token, and (the SetupInstructions block below already renders the paste-ready per-client config). Keep the existing branches honest: 'env' → the existing 'Environment variable' badge (the local token does not apply; clients use MCP_API_KEY); the default 'user-keys' branch → the existing personal-API-key guidance (login on; no token shown); add a degenerate 'none' branch → an honest 'Couldn't generate a local token' note (should not occur in normal boot). The header status badge (statusBadge, ~lines 68-82) already maps authConfigured → green 'Enabled' / amber 'No auth'; since 'local-token' has authConfigured true it reads green 'Enabled', and only the degenerate 'none' (authConfigured false) reads amber 'No auth' — confirm this and cover it with a test (no badge-logic change expected). All microcopy follows the writing-for-humans skill. Tests — extend apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/__tests__/ExternalMcpCard.test.tsx (each a purpose comment): the 'local-token' branch renders the token + Copy; the SetupInstructions snippet embeds the real token (assert the rendered snippet contains the token, not the placeholder); the 'env' branch hides the token and shows the env badge; the 'user-keys' branch hides the token and shows the personal-key guidance; the degenerate 'none' shows the honest fallback; the header badge is green 'Enabled' for local-token and amber 'No auth' only for 'none'. Add a snippet-shape unit test apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/__tests__/external-mcp-snippets.test.ts (create if absent): buildSnippets(endpoint, token) embeds `Bearer <token>`; buildSnippets(endpoint, null) embeds the placeholder. Acceptance: client typecheck + tests pass; the token is shown and copyable in local-token mode; env/user-keys/none states are honest. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck && pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/__tests__/ExternalMcpCard.test.tsx apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/__tests__/external-mcp-snippets.test.ts. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 4 (Client — ExternalMcpCard, honest states), §User Experience, §Acceptance Criteria (Server settings tab). Depends on 3.1 (the DTO type + populated fields). Runs in parallel with all of Phase 2. NOTE: if this exceeds one focused pass, the Rotate action is already carved out into 3.3 — keep this task display + snippets + honest states only.
tasks.activeForm: Building the local-token settings display and honest states

tasks.subject: [mcp-local-auth-posture] [P3] Transport.rotateMcpLocalToken (+ Http/Direct/mock impls) + Rotate button with breaks-clients confirm + tests
tasks.description: Add the rotate action end to end. Add rotateMcpLocalToken(): Promise<{ localToken: string }> to the Transport interface in packages/shared/src/transport.ts (near getConfig/updateConfig, ~lines 806-809) with TSDoc (regenerate the per-instance local MCP token; existing configured clients stop working until they re-paste it). This is a REQUIRED interface member, so implement it in every concrete Transport and the mock or the client/test-utils build breaks: HttpTransport (apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/transport/http-transport.ts) — POST /api/config/mcp/rotate-token and return the parsed { localToken }, mirroring the existing getConfig/updateConfig fetch helpers in this file; DirectTransport (apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/direct/system-methods.ts, alongside getConfig ~line 597) — call the in-process rotate path the same way getConfig reads config in-process (invoke the server rotate service / rotateMcpLocalToken(dorkHome) from '@dorkos/server' token module wired through the embedded server) and return { localToken }; keep it compile-safe and honest (the embedded Obsidian settings surface may not expose the button, but the interface member must exist); mock transport in packages/test-utils/src/mock-factories.ts (~line 452, beside updateConfig) — add rotateMcpLocalToken: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({ localToken: 'dork_mcp_local_test' }). Rotate button in ExternalMcpCard.tsx's 'local-token' McpAuthRow (from task 3.2): a 'Rotate' button that opens a confirm dialog (an AlertDialog / confirm primitive from '@/layers/shared/ui') whose copy plainly warns that rotating breaks existing configured clients until they re-paste the new token (writing-for-humans); on confirm, call const { localToken } = await transport.rotateMcpLocalToken() and invalidate the config query (queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['config'] })) so the new token + pre-filled snippets re-render. Tests — extend apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/__tests__/ExternalMcpCard.test.tsx (each a purpose comment, mock transport): clicking Rotate opens the confirm; confirming calls transport.rotateMcpLocalToken and invalidates the config query; the confirm copy warns that existing clients break. Acceptance: shared + client + test-utils typecheck; every implementor of the new required Transport member compiles; Rotate → confirm → transport call → config refresh works. Verify: pnpm --filter @dorkos/shared build && pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/test-utils typecheck && pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/__tests__/ExternalMcpCard.test.tsx. Cites: spec §Detailed Design 4 (Rotate button / rotate action), §User Experience (Rotate), §Acceptance Criteria. Depends on 3.2 (shares ExternalMcpCard.tsx — sequential) and 1.1 (rotateMcpLocalToken for the DirectTransport in-process path). Runs in parallel with all of Phase 2.
tasks.activeForm: Wiring the rotate transport method and Rotate button

tasks.subject: [mcp-local-auth-posture] [P4] Docs: MCP/self-hosting token step + A2A auth note (demo-claim-gate honest)
tasks.description: Update the user-facing docs that cover adding the DorkOS MCP to an external client, and the A2A auth requirement. Search docs/ for the existing self-hosting / external-MCP / MCP-integration pages (grep for 'External MCP', 'mcpServers', 'Authorization', or the /mcp endpoint). On the page that documents wiring an external MCP client, add the one-time Authorization: Bearer header step and where to find the token: Settings → Server → External MCP, or the mcp-local-token file in the DorkOS data folder. On the A2A docs, note that A2A JSON-RPC now needs the same token in login-off mode — document ONLY the auth requirement; do NOT assert Mesh+Relay / A2A works end-to-end (demo-claim gate, AGENTS.md — describe the auth step, not a working multi-agent demo). All prose follows the writing-for-humans skill (9th-grade level, benefit-first, no hype, honesty gate). If a new doc page is added, register it in contributing/INDEX.md and confirm docs:coverage stays green. Acceptance: the token step and A2A auth note are documented honestly; no unverified end-to-end A2A claim; docs:coverage green if a page was added. Verify: pnpm docs:coverage (and a manual writing-for-humans five-check pass). Cites: spec §Documentation. Depends on 2.3 and 3.3 (so the documented steps match the shipped behavior and UI). Priority medium.

tasks.subject: [mcp-local-auth-posture] [P4] Changelog fragment — breaking hard-cut (external MCP/A2A need the local token in login-off mode)
tasks.description:
Write the user-facing changelog fragment for this breaking behavior change. Create changelog/unreleased/<id>-mcp-local-auth-posture.md where <id> is a timestamp id from node .claude/scripts/id.ts (filename <YYMMDD-HHMMSS>-mcp-local-auth-posture.md per changelog/README.md). No frontmatter; use Keep-a-Changelog headings (### Security and ### Changed fit this change). Follow the writing-for-humans skill: plain enough for a smart 9th grader, benefit-first, name the outcome, no hype, honesty gate (do NOT claim Mesh+Relay/A2A works end-to-end — describe only the auth requirement). Convey plainly: DorkOS now protects the tools that change things on your machine. When you have not turned on login, those tools — and agent-to-agent calls — need a token you paste into your MCP client once. You find it in Settings → Server → External MCP, or in the mcp-local-token file in your DorkOS data folder. Read-only checks (health, listing tools) still work with no token. This is a hard change with no grace period: existing MCP and A2A clients must add the token to keep using the tools that change things. Reference (DOR-278). Example shape (rewrite in your own plain words, keep it honest):
### Security
- Close a gap where any program on your computer could run DorkOS tools that change things without a token. With login off, those tools and agent-to-agent calls now need a one-time token you paste into your MCP client. Find it in Settings → Server → External MCP. (DOR-278)
### Changed
- External MCP and A2A clients now need your local token when login is off. Health checks and listing tools still work without one; there is no grace period. (DOR-278)
Acceptance: the fragment exists with a valid timestamp-id filename; it passes the five writing-for-humans self-checks; it makes no unverified end-to-end claim. Verify: the fragment is valid markdown and reads at a 9th-grade level (manual writing-for-humans review; there is no automated test for prose). Cites: spec §Documentation (Changelog fragment), changelog/README.md, writing-for-humans skill. Depends on 2.2 (the breaking behavior) and 3.1 (the settings surface it points at). Priority medium.

tasks.subject: [mcp-local-auth-posture] [P4] Draft ADR — default-on local MCP token with a readOnlyHint capability carve-out
tasks.description: Draft the proposed ADR the spec calls for (§Related ADRs). Prefer running /adr:from-spec against specs/mcp-local-auth-posture/02-specification.md (it applies the significance rubric at extraction and writes decisions/<id>-<slug>.md + the decisions/manifest.json entry using a timestamp id from .claude/scripts/id.ts); otherwise write it by hand following the writing-adrs skill (concise, decision-focused). Title: 'Default-on local MCP token with a readOnlyHint capability carve-out'. Capture: the per-instance 0600 local token (the Jupyter pattern), generated once at boot in login-off mode and stored under the dork home; the fail-safe read-only carve-out keyed off readOnlyHint (fail-closed — unannotated or non-read-only tools are guarded); the hard-cut rollout (no warn-then-enforce); and A2A execution always token-gated in login-off mode (agent-card GET discovery stays open). State that it SUPERSEDES ADR-0103's zero-config passthrough branch and EXTENDS ADR-0320's login-off path (the token is inactive when login is on, yielding to per-user keys). Cross-link ADR-0103, ADR-0320, ADR-0227, ADR-0315, ADR-0311. Acceptance: decisions/<id>-<slug>.md and its manifest.json entry exist; context, decision, consequences, and superseded/related links are captured. Verify: the ADR renders; the id came from .claude/scripts/id.ts; decisions/manifest.json is valid JSON. Cites: spec §Related ADRs (Proposed ADR). Depends on 2.2 (the decision is realized in the middleware rewrite). Priority low.
